acceptance_targets: [chapter_17_F002] PASS if in each cited span there is no run longer than 6 consecutive paragraphs without Ina thought/sensation/self-assessment, and each span includes at least 2 explicit interior beats tied to live testimony/ruling content; otherwise FAIL.

[chapter_17_F003] PASS if references to Ji-eun's left-hand tremor/stillness occur no more than 2 times across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:157-475 and at least 2 distinct alternate stress cues are visible; FAIL if the tremor cue remains the dominant repeated marker.

[chapter_17_F004] PASS if the exact gaze-pivot template appears at most twice in the full revised chapter and at least two of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:101/:167/:319 are rewritten with different beat mechanics; otherwise FAIL.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in each cited span there is no run longer than 6 consecutive paragraphs without Ina thought/sensation/self-assessment, and each span includes at least 2 explicit interior beats tied to live testimony/ruling content; otherwise FAIL.
findings.problem: Two long stretches run as near-transcript without focalizer interiority (Ji-eun cross-exam and indictment procedure), pushing close-third narration toward screenplay mode.
findings.rewrite_direction: Insert Ina's internal experience beats inside the cited spans: one around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:285-303 during debt/sedation testimony, and one around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:391-401 when indictment conditions are read and processed.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if references to Ji-eun's left-hand tremor/stillness occur no more than 2 times across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:157-475 and at least 2 distinct alternate stress cues are visible; FAIL if the tremor cue remains the dominant repeated marker.
findings.problem: Ji-eun's stress is repeatedly signaled with the same left-hand tremor cue, creating mechanical repetition from a single stress marker and flattening emotional progression.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one early tremor anchor near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:157 or :267, then replace later repeats near snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:321 and :475 with different embodied signals (voice break, breath control failure, posture shift, forced smile collapse) consistent with her public/private register toggling.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the exact gaze-pivot template appears at most twice in the full revised chapter and at least two of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:101/:167/:319 are rewritten with different beat mechanics; otherwise FAIL.
findings.problem: The recurring gaze-pivot construction ("looked ... then back/then at ...") appears three times, becoming a visible prose tic and reducing line-level freshness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least two of the cited lines so turn-taking is conveyed through non-gaze actions (object handling, interruption, breath, micro-movement) while preserving scene logic.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
